title: Freehand: the skeleton, from memory
testCase: Is there a DOCTYPE, html, head with a title, and body with content?
blocks.0.text: Empty editor. No hints in the code. No dashes to fill in.
blocks.2.text: Write the whole page for your topic — from scratch, from memory. The skeleton, the name in the tab, the line at the very top, the heading and a few sentences.
blocks.3.text: Do not go back and copy from earlier lessons. Try on your own first. You will get something wrong — that is part of the exercise.
blocks.5.text: There is a difference between "I understand it when I see it" and "I can write it". The first is a feeling. The second is a skill. Only one of them will help you in front of an empty file on a Monday morning.
blocks.6.text: If you manage without looking back — you know the skeleton. If not — go back, look, and try again tomorrow. That is not failure, that is how memory works.
